calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch appeared by zoom for Petitioners/Trustees.

The Court expressed concern about the stale accounting noting that it was filed more than one year after the account was closed. Mr. Urbatsch stated that there was only about $13,000.00 left at that time, that they will do a cash reconciliation and that there's not enough money to pay for fees. Discussion was held regarding the disrespectful tone that was expressed towards the Probate Examiner in the supplement that was written by Mr. Urbatsch. The Fifth and Final Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-16-2021 Continued To Aug-18-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Aug-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Farinas appeared by telephone for Gabriela Martes and Suzy Martes.

The Court noted that there has been repeated co-mingling of funds and mishandling of the special needs funds, and that it raises the question of whether some form of protection such as a blocked account needs to be put in place. Ms. Farinas stated that her Clients are not professionals, that her Clients are doing their best, that no other family member wants to do this and that because of the amount, she does not think they will be able to get a professional fiduciary. Ms. Farinas also stated that a blocked account could impact Mr. Arevalo's public benefits, that she has provided a check list and is guiding her Clients, that they are keeping closer tabs on what is happening with the account and that efforts have been made to stop this from happening in the future. In addition, Ms. Farinas stated that all of the funds that were taken in error have been re-paid. The Court raised the issue of the Trustees using the account for their benefit, noted that there is not a lot of money left in the account, raised the issue of attorney's fees for appearances on dates when there was no hearing and fees for making and cancelling courtcall, and questions why those fees should be charged to the trust. Ms. Farinas stated that preparing for the hearing occurred prior to being aware that the hearing was not going forward, that courtcall requires that they obtain approval by the Clerk and that it requires extra time and special handling. Ms. Farinas also stated that proof of the $52.00 repayment and the $20.00 discrepancy are reflected in the account statement in the second supplement. The Fourth Account and Report of Trustees, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
